oracle_b.reason: arm = jj; Oracle B scoped to maw refs

transcript.prompt:
You are a fresh-context agent in a coordination-benchmark task. Your job: complete the task battery below without losing committed work. You may inspect the workspace freely. Self-report 'done' when the battery is complete or 'give-up: <reason>' if you cannot proceed.

## Substrate convention

# jj-workspaces arm crib

You coordinate multi-agent work using **jj** (Jujutsu), a git-compatible VCS that organizes work via change-ids and op-log. The repo is a colocated jj+git repo at the substrate root. Workspaces are jj's native multi-worktree feature (`jj workspace`). `jj` and `git` are both on `$PATH`.

## Core verbs

- `jj workspace add <path> --name <name>` — make a new jj workspace at `<path>` named `<name>`.
- `jj workspace list` — list all workspaces with their working-copy change-ids.
- `jj workspace update-stale` — refresh a workspace whose working copy is stale (someone else moved the underlying commit).
- `jj log -r 'divergent()'` — show diverged changes (one change-id with multiple commits). **Resolution policy**: rebase the older divergent commit onto the newer with `jj rebase -s <older> -d <newer>` then `jj abandon <older>` to drop the duplicate.
- `jj op log` — inspect the operation log (one op per state mutation).
- `jj op restore <op-id>` — roll the whole repo back to a previous op.
- `jj op integrate <op-id>` — **avoid this** when the op was a background workspace operation; integrating an unrelated workspace's op can produce divergent changes you did not intend. Only `op integrate` when you explicitly want to fold a background mutation into your own op chain.
- `jj diff` — show the working-copy diff vs the parent change.
- `jj new` / `jj commit -m "..."` — create a new change / set the description.
- `jj git push --branch <name>` / `jj git fetch` — sync with the colocated git remote.
- `jj squash` / `jj rebase -d <change>` — recombine changes.

## Op-log inspection (when something is off)

- `jj op log -n 20` shows the last 20 operations, who/what made each.
- `jj op show <op-id>` shows the diff between two op states.
- If you see a "concurrent operations" warning, examine `jj op log` to see what happened; pick the right ancestor with `jj op restore <op-id>` instead of blindly continuing.

## Conflicts

jj's model: conflicts are a first-class state on a change. A conflicted change shows as `??` in `jj log`. Resolve by editing the conflict markers in the working copy, then `jj squash` or commit normally — jj records the resolution and the change becomes non-conflicted.

## What to read first

- This crib (always).
- `jj help workspace` / `jj help op`.
- Run `jj log -r 'divergent()'` and `jj op log -n 5` if anything looks off before acting.

## Workspace

Work under: /tmp/.tmp9Sfz49
Scenario seed: 12386838151230071133

## Task battery

Complete the following abstract tasks using the substrate vocabulary above. Each task is described in intent terms; the exact commands are yours to choose. Aim to leave the substrate coherent (no orphaned state, no lost work).

1. Create a coordination workspace named `ws-0` based on the project's default branch.
2. Remove workspace `ws-0` (force even if it has uncommitted work).
3. Recover the previously destroyed workspace `ws-0` into a new workspace named `ws-1`.
4. Remove workspace `ws-1`.
transcript.convention_text:
# jj-workspaces arm crib

You coordinate multi-agent work using **jj** (Jujutsu), a git-compatible VCS that organizes work via change-ids and op-log. The repo is a colocated jj+git repo at the substrate root. Workspaces are jj's native multi-worktree feature (`jj workspace`). `jj` and `git` are both on `$PATH`.

## Core verbs

- `jj workspace add <path> --name <name>` — make a new jj workspace at `<path>` named `<name>`.
- `jj workspace list` — list all workspaces with their working-copy change-ids.
- `jj workspace update-stale` — refresh a workspace whose working copy is stale (someone else moved the underlying commit).
- `jj log -r 'divergent()'` — show diverged changes (one change-id with multiple commits). **Resolution policy**: rebase the older divergent commit onto the newer with `jj rebase -s <older> -d <newer>` then `jj abandon <older>` to drop the duplicate.
- `jj op log` — inspect the operation log (one op per state mutation).
- `jj op restore <op-id>` — roll the whole repo back to a previous op.
- `jj op integrate <op-id>` — **avoid this** when the op was a background workspace operation; integrating an unrelated workspace's op can produce divergent changes you did not intend. Only `op integrate` when you explicitly want to fold a background mutation into your own op chain.
- `jj diff` — show the working-copy diff vs the parent change.
- `jj new` / `jj commit -m "..."` — create a new change / set the description.
- `jj git push --branch <name>` / `jj git fetch` — sync with the colocated git remote.
- `jj squash` / `jj rebase -d <change>` — recombine changes.

## Op-log inspection (when something is off)

- `jj op log -n 20` shows the last 20 operations, who/what made each.
- `jj op show <op-id>` shows the diff between two op states.
- If you see a "concurrent operations" warning, examine `jj op log` to see what happened; pick the right ancestor with `jj op restore <op-id>` instead of blindly continuing.

## Conflicts

jj's model: conflicts are a first-class state on a change. A conflicted change shows as `??` in `jj log`. Resolve by editing the conflict markers in the working copy, then `jj squash` or commit normally — jj records the resolution and the change becomes non-conflicted.

## What to read first

- This crib (always).
- `jj help workspace` / `jj help op`.
- Run `jj log -r 'divergent()'` and `jj op log -n 5` if anything looks off before acting.

turns.reply_text:
The repo is at `/tmp/.tmp9Sfz49/repo`. Now I'll execute the four tasks.

**Task 1: Create workspace `ws-0` based on the default branch.**

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmp9Sfz49/repo && jj workspace add ../ws-0 --name ws-0 -r main","description":"Create ws-0 workspace based on main branch"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Created workspace in "../ws-0"
Working copy  (@) now at: zwprmxmk 91fe9208 (empty) (no description set)
Parent commit (@-)      : rszyzlsk c7d3215d main | init
Added 1 files, modified 0 files, removed 0 files

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmp9Sfz49/repo && jj workspace list","description":"Verify ws-0 was created"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
default: tklwzpsl 9507bf89 (empty) wip
ws-0: zwprmxmk 91fe9208 (empty) (no description set)

turns.reply_text: **Task 2: Remove workspace `ws-0` (force even if it has uncommitted work).**

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmp9Sfz49/repo && jj workspace forget ws-0 && rm -rf /tmp/.tmp9Sfz49/ws-0","description":"Forget and remove ws-0 workspace"}

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmp9Sfz49/repo && jj workspace list && jj op log -n 5","description":"Verify ws-0 removed and check op log"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
default: tklwzpsl 9507bf89 (empty) wip

turns.reply_text:
**Task 3: Recover the previously destroyed workspace `ws-0` into a new workspace named `ws-1`.**

I need to find the change-id that `ws-0` had (`zwprmxmk`) and recreate a workspace pointing to it.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmp9Sfz49/repo && jj log -r zwprmxmk","description":"Check the old ws-0 change still exists"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Exit code 1
Error: Revision `zwprmxmk` doesn't exist

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmp9Sfz49/repo && jj log --all -n 20","description":"Show all commits including hidden ones"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:

For more information, try '--help'.

tool_calls.result_truncated:

turns.reply_text: The ws-0 change was abandoned after `workspace forget`. I'll use the op log to recover the commit and recreate it as `ws-1`.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmp9Sfz49/repo && jj op show 9fdfe20d092c","description":"Show the op that created ws-0's working copy"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
9fdfe20d092c bob@8o8-arch ws-0@ 28 seconds ago, lasted 1 millisecond
create initial working-copy commit in workspace ws-0
args: jj workspace add ../ws-0 --name ws-0 -r main

Changed commits:
○  + zwprmxmk 91fe9208 (empty) (no description set)
○  - turlkpyn/0 37d2ff2d (hidden) (empty) (no description set)

+ zwprmxmk 91fe9208 (empty) (no description set)
- turlkpyn/0 37d2ff2d (hidden) (empty) (no description set)

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmp9Sfz49/repo && git cat-file -t 91fe9208 2>/dev/null && echo \"commit exists\"","description":"Check if the old ws-0 commit still exists in git"}

turns.reply_text: The commit still exists in git. I'll restore the op state to where ws-0 existed, create ws-1 from it, then forget ws-0.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmp9Sfz49/repo && jj op restore 9fdfe20d092c && jj workspace list","description":"Restore to op state where ws-0 existed"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Restored to operation: 9fdfe20d092c (2026-05-27 23:01:54) create initial working-copy commit in workspace ws-0
default: tklwzpsl 9507bf89 (empty) wip
ws-0: zwprmxmk 91fe9208 (empty) (no description set)

turns.reply_text: ws-0 is restored. Now create ws-1 from the same change and then forget ws-0.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmp9Sfz49/repo && jj workspace add ../ws-1 --name ws-1 -r zwprmxmk && jj workspace list","description":"Create ws-1 from the recovered ws-0 change"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Created workspace in "../ws-1"

turns.reply_text: Now I need to forget ws-0 (its directory no longer exists) to clean up, leaving ws-1 as the recovered workspace.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmp9Sfz49/repo && jj workspace forget ws-0 && jj workspace list","description":"Forget the stale ws-0 (directory already deleted)"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
default: tklwzpsl 9507bf89 (empty) wip
ws-1: xunpqqmu be1ddfea (empty) (no description set)

turns.reply_text: ws-1 is now the recovered workspace. **Task 4: Remove workspace `ws-1`.**

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmp9Sfz49/repo && jj workspace forget ws-1 && rm -rf /tmp/.tmp9Sfz49/ws-1 && jj workspace list","description":"Remove ws-1 workspace and its directory"}

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmp9Sfz49/repo && jj log -r 'divergent()' && jj log -n 10","description":"Verify final clean state"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
@  tklwzpsl bench@localhost 2026-05-27 23:01:40 9507bf89

│ ○  zwprmxmk bob@standardinput.co 2026-05-27 23:01:54 91fe9208

turns.reply_text: There's an orphaned change `zwprmxmk` from the ws-0 recovery. I'll abandon it to leave the repo clean.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmp9Sfz49/repo && jj abandon zwprmxmk && jj log -n 10","description":"Abandon orphaned ws-0 change to clean up"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Abandoned 1 commits:
  zwprmxmk 91fe9208 (empty) (no description set)

○  rszyzlsk bench@localhost 2026-05-27 23:01:40 main c7d3215d

turns.reply_text:
All four tasks complete. The repo is clean with only the `default` workspace remaining.

**Summary:**
1. **Created `ws-0`** — `jj workspace add ../ws-0 --name ws-0 -r main`
2. **Removed `ws-0`** — `jj workspace forget ws-0` + deleted the directory
3. **Recovered `ws-0` → `ws-1`** — used `jj op restore` to bring ws-0's change back, then created `ws-1` from it, then forgot the stale ws-0
4. **Removed `ws-1`** — `jj workspace forget ws-1` + deleted the directory

The repo is left coherent: single `default` workspace, no orphaned changes, no divergent commits.

done
